title: Nexus Financial Transaction
description: Nexus unified financial transaction data contract. Nexus is NOT a ledger — it describes financial events (trades, legs, fees) across all Transactional Ledger pillars, not balance states. One Nexus transaction corresponds to one Linking Engine action_id. Partial pillar data is normal: legs are added incrementally as pillar data arrives.

transaction_id.description: Unique Nexus transaction identifier, derived from the Linking Engine action_id (e.g. the action_id value itself).

parent_transaction_id.description: Groups transactions within a payment lifecycle (e.g. auth → capture → refund). Maps from LE action_root_id. Allows consumers to reconstruct the full lifecycle of a payment.

action_id.description: Raw Linking Engine action_id preserved for traceability. This is the primary correlation key between Nexus and LE.

action_root_id.description: Raw Linking Engine action_root_id preserved for traceability. Groups multiple action_ids that belong to the same payment lifecycle.

status.description: Technical data-completeness status of the transaction. NOT_LIVE: insufficient pillar data for required trades. LIVE: all required pillar data is present. DEAD: all data received and value dates have passed. The threshold for each status is configurable per trade family.

entity.description: The client entity that owns this transaction. The id field is the client identifier (client_id); sub_id is the sub-entity for platform clients (e.g. a Shopify sub-merchant).

cko_entity_id.description: CKO legal entity that holds the commercial relationship with the client. Other CKO entities involved in the transaction appear as party_id values on individual legs.

processed_at.description: Transaction processing timestamp, sourced from Gateway processed_on.

period_start.description: Billing period start date. Populated only for GLOBAL_PRICING trades (MinimumBilling, TieredPricing). Null/absent for transactional trades.

period_end.description: Billing period end date. Populated only for GLOBAL_PRICING trades (MinimumBilling, TieredPricing). Null/absent for transactional trades.

trades.description: Array of trades within this transaction. Each trade represents a distinct financial event (e.g. a capture, a refund, a scheme settlement).

Entity.description: Client entity identifier. Represents the client (not CKO) side of the commercial relationship.

sub_id.description: Sub-entity identifier for platform clients (e.g. a Shopify sub-merchant ID).

Trade.description: A trade represents a single financial event within a Nexus transaction. Trade ID convention: {action_id}_1, {action_id}_2, etc.

x-trade-type-matrix.description: Defines valid combinations of trade_family, trade_type, and trade_status. Only the combinations listed here are valid.

x-leg-composition-rules.description: Documents the expected leg composition per trade family/type. These are business rules, not enforced by JSON Schema validation. Cross-entity captures produce a separate TRANSFER trade rather than an additional leg.

legs: Cross-entity: additional TRANSFER trade (separate trade, not additional leg)

trade_id.description: Unique trade identifier within this Nexus transaction. Convention: {action_id}_1, {action_id}_2, etc.

trade_family.description: The product family this trade belongs to. Determines which trade_type and trade_status values are valid (see x-trade-type-matrix).

trade_type.description: The type of financial event within the trade family. Valid values depend on trade_family (see x-trade-type-matrix).

trade_status.description: Current status of the trade. Valid values depend on trade_family + trade_type (see x-trade-type-matrix).

trade_amount.description: Principal amount of the trade in trade_currency.

trade_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the trade amount.

metadata.description: Optional bag of trade-type-specific fields. Which fields are populated varies by trade family.

legs.description: Array of legs within this trade. Each leg represents a movement of value between two parties.

TradeMetadata.description: Optional trade-level metadata. All fields are optional; which fields are populated depends on the trade family. See x-relevant-families on each field for guidance.

scheme_code.description: Scheme identifier code (e.g. VISA, MC, AMEX).

payment_method.description: Payment method used for the transaction (e.g. card, bank_transfer).

acquirer_name.description: Name of the acquirer that processed the transaction.

acquirer_country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the acquirer.

cash_batch_id.description: Cash batch identifier used as the Cash Matching key.

client_settlement_type.description: Whether the client is settled gross or net.

merchant_category_code.description: Merchant Category Code (MCC) for the transaction.

source_event_type.description: Original event type from the source system (e.g. Gateway event_type).

invoice_number.description: Invoice number associated with the transaction.

settlement_service_name.description: Name of the settlement service used (e.g. ISS, UKNNSS).

settlement_country_code.description: Country code of the settlement service.

central_processing_date.description: Date the transaction was cleared at the scheme (central processing date).

enhanced_action_type.description: Enhanced action type providing additional detail (e.g. PartialRefund).

reconciliation_reference.description: Reconciliation reference that matches Predicted/Async/Actual/Sweep COS records.

scheme_partner_identifier.description: BID/ICA-level scheme identifier for the partner.

payout_id.description: Payout identifier for payout and cash trades.

billing_descriptor.description: Billing descriptor shown on client statements.

is_net_settled.description: Whether this transaction is net-settled at the scheme level.

Leg.description: A leg represents a movement of value between two parties within a trade. Leg ID convention: {trade_id}_L1, {trade_id}_L2, etc. Partial pillar data is normal — legs are added as pillars arrive.

leg_id.description: Unique leg identifier within this trade. Convention: {trade_id}_L{n}.

leg_type.description: Type of value movement this leg represents. AUTH_HOLD: authorization hold. SCHEME_SETTLEMENT: movement between scheme and CKO entity. FUNDING: movement between CKO entity and client entity. ENTITY_TRANSFER: movement between CKO entities. FX_CONVERSION: currency conversion within a CKO entity.

leg_amount.description: Gross amount of the leg in leg_currency.

leg_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the leg amount.

leg_status.description: Whether this leg is predicted or actual. COS-sourced legs are PREDICTED; SD-sourced legs are ACTUAL.

value_date.description: Value date for this leg. For FUNDING: future available date from FIAPI Pending event (arrears logic). For SCHEME_SETTLEMENT: SD expected_value_date. For AUTH_HOLD: trade_date.

from_party.description: The party sending value in this leg.

to_party.description: The party receiving value in this leg.

reserve_amount.description: Rolling reserve amount withheld. Only present on FUNDING legs for ACQUIRING CAPTURE trades. Fixed reserve and collateral movements are represented as separate TRANSFER trades.

reserve_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the reserve_amount.

fees.description: Array of fees on this leg. May be empty. For passthrough fees, the same fee_id appears on both the SCHEME_SETTLEMENT and FUNDING legs.

Party.description: Identifies a party in a leg. Parties are one of: SCHEME (card network), CKO_ENTITY (Checkout.com legal entity), or CLIENT_ENTITY (the client).

party_type.description: Type of the party. SCHEME: card network (e.g. Visa, Mastercard). CKO_ENTITY: a Checkout.com legal entity. CLIENT_ENTITY: the client.

party_id.description: Identifier of the party (e.g. VISA, CKO_UK_LTD, cli_acme_corp).

currency_account_id.description: NAS currency account identifier. Null/absent for SCHEME parties.

Fee.description: A fee associated with a leg. Fee ID convention: {leg_id}_F{n}. For passthrough fees (scheme to client), the same fee_id is used on both the SCHEME_SETTLEMENT and FUNDING legs.

fee_id.description: Unique fee identifier within this leg. Convention: {leg_id}_F{n}. Shared across SCHEME_SETTLEMENT and FUNDING legs for passthrough fees.

fee_type.description: Type of fee. This enum is extensible — additional fee types may be added in future versions.

fee_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the fee amount.

fee_status.description: Whether this fee is predicted or actual.

passthrough.description: True if this fee is passed through from scheme to client. When true, the same fee_id appears on both the SCHEME_SETTLEMENT and FUNDING legs.

fee_amount_type.description: Whether the fee is a fixed amount or a variable (percentage-based) amount.

tax_amount.description: Tax component of the fee, if applicable.

tax_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the tax_amount.
